text.title: BA Planning Monitoring and Performance
text.summary: Per-task monitoring cadences, replan trigger conditions, performance metric requirements for T5, and common LLM failure modes in KA1 planning.

rule.statement: Assign different review cadences per task; do not use a single global review frequency for all five KA1 artifacts.
rule.rationale: Performance metrics (T5) go stale weekly; stakeholder and governance artifacts drift fortnightly; approach and information management artifacts are stable monthly. A single cadence either over-reviews stable artifacts or under-reviews volatile ones.

list.item: T5 Performance Improvements: weekly cadence — compute BA metrics and emit CR patches if triggers fire.
T2 Stakeholder Engagement: fortnightly cadence — reconcile stakeholder list against project actuals.
T1 Approach, T3 Governance, T4 Information Management: monthly cadence.

rule.statement: Encode replan triggers as machine-readable YAML in triggers.yml, not prose; prose triggers only fire when a human reads and interprets them.
rule.rationale: The monitor agent reads trigger conditions automatically; prose conditions require an LLM to interpret them, introducing interpretation errors and silent misses.

list.item: Stakeholder added or removed from the project.
Scope change request merged into the requirements baseline.
Two or more missed elicitation sessions within one cadence period.
Performance metric breach (a KPI missing its threshold for two consecutive reviews).
last_reviewed date older than the task's cadence threshold.

rule.statement: List at least three measurable BA performance metrics in T5 on the first cycle with baseline=null and target=null; do not invent baselines before two cycles of data exist.
rule.rationale: Invented baselines are post-hoc rationalizations — the first cycle is for measurement establishment, the second for improvement comparison. Skipping T5 entirely means no monitoring loop exists.

example.note: T5 first-cycle metrics: (1) requirement defect density = defects found in validation / total requirements; baseline: null; target: null. (2) elicitation throughput = requirements documented per session; baseline: null. (3) rework rate = requirements changed after baseline / total baselined; baseline: null.

antipattern.description: Omitting T5 on the first cycle because "we don't have baselines yet."
antipattern.reason: Without T5, there is no instrumentation; the second cycle cannot improve because it has nothing to improve from. Baselines are collected during the first cycle, not before it.

antipattern.description: LLM merges the five BABOK KA1 tasks into a single "BA Plan" document and silently drops T4 and T5.
antipattern.reason: Information management and performance improvement are the least visible KA1 tasks; LLMs drop them because they appear in fewer training examples. Validate output against the five-task schema; reject if any task is missing.

antipattern.description: LLM produces vague governance approvers ("leadership," "the product team") in T3.
antipattern.reason: Vague approvers cannot sign off on anything; escalation paths point to a role, not a person. Require an email or @-handle; reject role-only outputs.

antipattern.description: LLM rewrites full artifact content during monitoring instead of emitting CR patches.
antipattern.reason: Full-doc overwrites destroy the approver's ability to review changes incrementally; CR patches force explicit diff review. Cap monitor agent at 5 changes per artifact per run.
